w:document.mc:Ignorable: w14 w15 w16se w16cid w16 w16cex w16sdtdh w16sdtfl w16du wp14

w:r.w:t: including lead, which is known to the State of California to cause cancer and birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to
Figure 3: Product dimension drawing of the JC02-1 module.
The module has overall dimensions of 27.5mm * 25mm * 12.8mm and weighs approximately 10.5g.
The module connects via a multi-colored wire harness. Ensure correct pin connections to avoid damage.
Figure 4: Pin function and wiring diagram for the JC02-1 module.
Insert the data cable into the distance measurement module.
Connect the module to a computer or other control device (e.g., microcontroller, FPV flight controller) via an appropriate adapter (e.g., USB-to-UART converter).
Ensure the power supply voltage is within 3.3V ~ 3.8V. Do not reverse the plug.
Install any necessary distance measurement software or drivers on your computer/control device.
Open the software interface and select the corresponding serial port number for communication.
The module communicates using a UART (TTL level) serial interface. Configure your control device's serial port with the following parameters:
The module responds to specific hexadecimal commands for various operations. All byte content is in hexadecimal.
No response packet is sent after a successful reset command. Wait 500ms for reinitialization.
Initiates a single distance measurement. The module performs one measurement and returns the data.
AE A7 17 00 85 MMSG* BC BE (where MMSG* is the returned measurement result).

The MMSG (Measurement Message) contains the measured value as a signed short type with the high 8 bits first and the low 8 bits last. Units: Angle: 0.1 degrees; Speed: 0.1 km/h; Distance: 0.1 meters (01 byte), 0.1 yards (02 byte), 0.1 feet (03 byte). Note: The device supports only one linear distance measurement. Other fields (Elevation, Sine Height, Azimuth, Span, Velocity, Parallax) are reserved for future updates and currently return 0.
AE A7 04 00 0E 12 BC BE (upon receiving the command, respond with this to initiate continuous measurement and respond upon successful measurement).
Table 7: Stop Continuous Ranging Control Command
